proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear relationship).
Female sex (sex_female=1) is associated with shorter pfs_months than male sex.
Higher ecog_ps is associated with shorter pfs_months (monotonic decreasing).
stage_iv=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0 patients.
has_brain_mets=1 patients have shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0 patients.
Mean pfs_months differs across smoking_status categories (former vs current vs never).
Squamous histology has shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.

analyses.result_summary: age_years is strongly positively correlated with pfs_months (r=+0.757, p≈0).
Females have a marginally shorter mean PFS than males (3.319 vs 3.523 months, diff=-0.204, p=2.4e-24).
Mean PFS decreases monotonically with ECOG: 4.327 (PS0), 3.185 (PS1), 2.152 (PS2); ANOVA p≈0.
stage_iv=1 has much shorter PFS than stage_iv=0 (2.925 vs 4.362, diff=-1.44, p≈0).
Brain mets patients have shorter PFS (2.743 vs 3.663, diff=-0.92, p≈0).
Current smokers have shorter PFS (mean 2.987) than former (3.607) or never smokers (3.675); ANOVA p=3.7e-187.
Adenocarcinoma PFS 3.670 vs squamous 2.813 (diff=+0.857, p≈0); squamous is worse.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
kras_g12c=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients.
alk_fusion=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients.
stk11_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients.
brca2_mutation=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
tmb_high=1 patients have a different mean pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months (linear relationship).

analyses.result_summary: EGFR-mutant slightly longer PFS (3.596 vs 3.407, diff=+0.189, p=1.9e-10).
KRAS G12C+ has substantially longer PFS overall (4.114 vs 3.332, diff=+0.782, p=4.8e-151) — but as later analyses show, this is driven entirely by sotorasib responders.
ALK fusion+ has shorter PFS (2.661 vs 3.472, diff=-0.810, p=2.9e-69).
STK11 has no detectable main effect on PFS (3.439 vs 3.430, p=0.75).
BRCA2 mutation associated with marginally shorter PFS (3.316 vs 3.435, diff=-0.119, p=0.041).
TMB-high modestly shorter PFS (3.296 vs 3.486, diff=-0.190, p=9.1e-18).
PDL1 TPS has essentially no linear association with PFS (r=+0.006, p=0.16).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have longer mean pfs_months than those not receiving it (overall main effect).

analyses.result_summary: No overall main effect of pembrolizumab on PFS (3.414 vs 3.449, diff=-0.035, p=0.081).
Sotorasib associated with longer PFS overall (3.641 vs 3.319, diff=+0.322, p=1.6e-53). Substantial and likely driven by KRAS G12C responders.
No overall main effect of olaparib (3.414 vs 3.439, diff=-0.025, p=0.25).
No overall main effect of osimertinib (3.434 vs 3.431, diff=+0.003, p=0.87).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Other routine labs (hemoglobin, alk phos, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium) have negligible associations with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Albumin positively correlates with PFS (r=+0.099, p=2.8e-109).
LDH weakly inversely correlates with PFS (r=-0.019, p=3.6e-5).
Weight loss inversely correlates with PFS (r=-0.127, p=7.5e-179).
CRP has no detectable association with PFS (r=+0.001, p=0.78).
NLR has no detectable association with PFS (r=+0.004, p=0.42).

analyses.code: scipy.stats.pearsonr for each lab vs pfs_months
analyses.result_summary: Hemoglobin, alkaline phos, AST, ALT, total bilirubin, creatinine, BUN, sodium, potassium, calcium each have |r|<0.01 with pfs_months and p>0.1; treated as non-prognostic in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a positive interaction between treatment_sotorasib and kras_g12c on pfs_months: sotorasib produces a much larger PFS gain in KRAS G12C+ patients than in KRAS G12C-negative patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_osimertinib and egfr_mutation on pfs_months: osimertinib produces a larger PFS gain in EGFR-mutant patients than in EGFR-wildtype patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_olaparib and brca2_mutation on pfs_months: olaparib produces a larger PFS gain in BRCA2-mutant patients than in BRCA2-wildtype patients.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and pdl1_tps on pfs_months: pembrolizumab produces a larger PFS gain at higher PD-L1 TPS.
There is a positive interaction between treatment_pembrolizumab and tmb_high on pfs_months: pembrolizumab produces a larger PFS gain in TMB-high patients.

analyses.result_summary: Strong positive interaction: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c coefficient = +2.558 months (p≈0). Stratified: KRAS G12C+ sotorasib effect = +2.55 months (p<1e-275); KRAS G12C- sotorasib effect = -0.005 (p=0.80).

No interaction: osimertinib:egfr_mutation coefficient = -0.020 (p=0.76). Stratified within EGFR+: osimertinib effect = -0.014 months (p=0.81), n_yes=1973. Osimertinib appears NOT to be effective in this cohort.
No interaction: olaparib:brca2_mutation coefficient = +0.037 (p=0.78). Within BRCA2+: olaparib effect = +0.010 months (p=0.93), n=417. Olaparib appears not to confer benefit even in BRCA2+ patients.
No interaction (coef=-0.116, p=0.22). Within pdl1>=0.5: pembrolizumab effect = -0.070 (p=0.11). No detectable PD-L1 modifier.
No interaction (coef=-0.018, p=0.68). Within TMB-high: pembrolizumab effect = -0.048 (p=0.20).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab is harmful (or less beneficial) in stk11_mutation=1 patients, even in PD-L1 high subset (negative interaction with STK11).
Pembrolizumab benefit is concentrated in patients with pdl1_tps>=0.5 AND tmb_high=1 AND stk11_mutation=0.
Osimertinib's lack of effect in EGFR+ patients persists when stratified by histology, smoking_status, has_brain_mets, stage_iv, ECOG, STK11, TMB and sex.
Olaparib's lack of effect in BRCA2+ patients persists across subgroup strata (sex, histology, stage, ECOG, smoking).

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab effect: stk11=0: -0.032 (p=0.14); stk11=1: -0.049 (p=0.34). No significant difference; pembrolizumab not beneficial in either group.

analyses.code: subset df where pdl1>=0.5 & tmb_high & stk11==0 then ttest
analyses.result_summary: Among pdl1>=0.5 & tmb_high & stk11==0 (n=2714), pembrolizumab effect = +0.056 months (p=0.52). No benefit even in this 'optimal' immuno subgroup.

analyses.code: exhaustive subgroup ttests on EGFR+ subset
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR+ (n=6517), osimertinib effect is null in every substratum: histology (adeno -0.045, squam +0.051), smoking (never -0.009, former +0.028, current -0.250 all NS), brain_mets, stage, STK11, TMB, sex (all |diff|<0.26, all p>0.05). Conclusion: osimertinib does not improve PFS in this dataset, even within EGFR+.

analyses.code: exhaustive subgroup ttests on BRCA2+ subset
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+ (n=1519), olaparib effect is null across all substrata; no subgroup with diff>+0.4 reaches significance. Olaparib does not improve PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The sotorasib treatment effect within KRAS G12C+ patients is larger in males (sex_female=0) than in females (sex_female=1) — a 3-way interaction sotorasib × kras_g12c × sex_female.

analyses.result_summary: Striking 3-way interaction. The treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c term is +4.663 (p≈0), and treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female is -4.688 (p≈0), so the effect among KRAS G12C+ females is essentially cancelled. Stratified means confirm: KRAS G12C+ males with sotorasib mean PFS=7.876 vs without=3.232 (diff=+4.644, p≈0, n=3508); KRAS G12C+ females with sotorasib=3.179 vs without=3.191 (diff=-0.012, p=0.88, n=2860).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Pembrolizumab effect on pfs_months differs by sex (treatment_pembrolizumab × sex_female interaction).
Osimertinib effect on pfs_months differs by sex within EGFR+ (treatment_osimertinib × sex_female interaction in EGFR-mutant subset).
Olaparib effect on pfs_months differs by sex within BRCA2+ (treatment_olaparib × sex_female interaction in BRCA2-mutant subset).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_pembrolizumab:sex_female coefficient = +0.020 (p=0.62). No sex modification of pembrolizumab effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols on EGFR+ subset with sex interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR+ subset, treatment_osimertinib:sex_female = +0.182 (p=0.12). Trend but not significant; no clear sex-specific osimertinib effect.

analyses.code: smf.ols on BRCA2+ subset with sex interaction
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+, treatment_olaparib:sex_female = +0.106 (p=0.66). No sex modification.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ males, sotorasib provides a +4 to +5 month PFS gain that persists after stratifying by stk11_mutation, tmb_high, has_brain_mets, and stage_iv.
Within KRAS G12C+ females, no measured covariate (stk11, tmb_high, brain_mets, stage_iv, histology, smoking) recovers a sotorasib benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Within KRAS G12C+ males (n=3508), sotorasib effect by substratum: stk11_mutation=0 +4.669 (p≈0); =1 +4.487 (p=4e-68); tmb_high=0 +4.631 (p≈0); =1 +4.660 (p=4e-173); has_brain_mets=0 +4.702; =1 +4.468; stage_iv=0 +4.709; =1 +4.623. Effect is uniformly large (+4 to +5) in every stratum.

Within KRAS G12C+ females (n=2860), no measured covariate produces a significant sotorasib benefit. Largest substratum effect (smoking_status=never, n=66) was -0.505 months (p=0.33). The null effect in females is robust.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a male-only analysis, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is large and positive (~+4.66).
In a female-only analysis, the treatment_sotorasib × kras_g12c interaction is essentially zero.

analyses.result_summary: Males only: treatment_sotorasib coefficient = -0.019 (p=0.50, in non-G12C); kras_g12c = -0.102 (p=0.03, untreated effect among males); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c = +4.663 (p≈0).
Females only: treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c = -0.024 (p=0.78). Confirms no sotorasib benefit in females, even in KRAS G12C+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable linear model adjusting for all features, treatment_sotorasib has a large positive coefficient driven entirely by the 3-way interaction with KRAS G12C and male sex; the other three treatments (pembrolizumab, olaparib, osimertinib) have null adjusted coefficients.

analyses.code: smf.ols(pfs_months ~ all_features + 4 treatments) and a version adding sotorasib×kras×sex 3-way
analyses.result_summary: Base multivariable model: treatment_sotorasib = +0.323 (p≈0); pembrolizumab = -0.006 (p=0.39); olaparib = +0.002 (p=0.74); osimertinib = +0.001 (p=0.88). After adding the sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female interaction, the main sotorasib coefficient drops to +0.002 (p=0.71) and the 3-way interaction term carries the effect (-4.581, p≈0 for the female-cancellation term). R² rises from 0.894 to 0.971 with the interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner causal forest (random forest treated/untreated models) followed by a regression tree on the patient-level CATE for treatment_sotorasib will identify KRAS G12C × male sex as the top splits, with mean predicted effect ~+4 to +5 months in that subgroup and near-zero elsewhere.
Causal-forest CATEs for pembrolizumab, osimertinib, and olaparib will have small magnitudes and no coherent subgroup with a strong, biologically plausible benefit.

analyses.code: T-learner with sklearn RandomForestRegressor for T=1 and T=0; DecisionTreeRegressor on CATE
analyses.result_summary: Sotorasib CATE: mean=+0.31, std=1.22, range [-3.0, +7.6]. Top tree splits: kras_g12c<=0.5 (mean CATE near 0 in all leaves), kras_g12c>0.5 splits on sex_female: sex_female<=0.5 (males) leaves: +4.28 to +5.48; sex_female>0.5 (females) leaves: -0.56 to +0.17. Confirms the male-only effect is dominant.

analyses.code: Same T-learner pipeline applied to pembrolizumab, osimertinib, olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab CATE std=0.23 (range [-1.4, +1.2]); leaves all near 0 (-0.25 to +0.23). Osimertinib CATE std=0.34 (range [-1.4, +1.6]); leaves -0.14 to +0.38, no biologically coherent subgroup. Olaparib CATE std=0.31; leaves -0.18 to +0.34. None of these treatments produces a meaningful subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP: treatment_sotorasib increases pfs_months by ~+4.6 months (95% CI excludes zero, p≈0) only in patients with kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 (male). In all other patients (kras_g12c=0 of either sex, or kras_g12c=1 females), sotorasib has no detectable PFS effect. The female-suppressed subgroup is the variable whose unfavorable value abolishes the treatment effect.
FINAL: treatment_pembrolizumab has no detectable PFS benefit in any tested subgroup of this dataset (including pdl1_tps>=0.5, tmb_high, stk11_mutation=0, and combinations).
FINAL: treatment_osimertinib has no detectable PFS benefit even in EGFR-mutant patients or any further-restricted subgroup.
FINAL: treatment_olaparib has no detectable PFS benefit even in BRCA2-mutant patients or any further-restricted subgroup.

analyses.code: Subset df to (kras_g12c==1) & (sex_female==0); ttest sotorasib vs not
analyses.result_summary: In KRAS G12C+ males (n=3508): sotorasib +4.644 months PFS (7.876 vs 3.232, p≈0). In KRAS G12C+ females (n=2860): -0.012 months (p=0.88). In KRAS G12C-negative males (n=24121): -0.019 (p=0.50). In KRAS G12C-negative females (n=19511): +0.012 (p=0.70). The complete subgroup definition is: kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0. Sex_female=1 is the unfavorable value that fully suppresses the otherwise large sotorasib effect.

analyses.code: Aggregate of subgroup ttests across all combinations of pdl1_tps>=0.5, tmb_high, stk11_mutation, sex_female, histology, ecog_ps
analyses.result_summary: No subgroup of pembrolizumab use produces a significant positive effect after multiple-comparison consideration. Most-favorable subgroup (pdl1>=0.5 AND tmb_high AND no stk11, n=2714) showed +0.056 months (p=0.52). Treatment_pembrolizumab is non-effective in this cohort.

analyses.code: Aggregate of subgroup ttests on EGFR+ stratified by sex, smoking, histology, brain_mets, stage_iv, ecog, stk11, tmb
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR+ no substratum yields a positive osimertinib effect; largest |effect| is -0.260 (TMB-high, p=0.07). Treatment_osimertinib is non-effective in this cohort, even in its 'natural' indication.

analyses.code: Aggregate of subgroup ttests on BRCA2+ stratified by sex, histology, stage, ECOG, smoking, brain_mets, STK11, TMB
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+ no substratum yields a positive olaparib effect; all p>0.09. Treatment_olaparib is non-effective in this cohort.
